title: Dakgalbi (Spicy Korean Stir-fried Chicken)
description: A bustling street-food icon from Chuncheon, Dakgalbi is a vibrant, communal masterpiece. Tender chicken thighs are marinated in a bold, crimson gochujang sauce and stir-fried with sweet potato, cabbage, and chewy rice cakes on a massive cast-iron griddle.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Chicken thighs","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"bite-sized pieces"},{"name":"Gochujang","qty":"3","unit":"tbsp","notes":"Korean red chili paste"},{"name":"Rice cakes (Tteok)","qty":"150","unit":"g","notes":"soaked in water"},{"name":"Sweet potato","qty":"1","unit":"medium","notes":"sliced into coins"},{"name":"Perilla leaves","qty":"10","unit":"pcs","notes":"torn for garnish"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Marinade**: In a large bowl, whisk together gochujang, soy sauce, minced garlic, ginger, and a touch of curry powder. Toss the chicken in this velvet-red paste and let it rest for 20 minutes.
2. **The Sizzle**: Heat a large cast-iron skillet with a splash of sesame oil. Sear the chicken until the edges caramelize and the sauce begins to bubble and thicken.
3. **The Veggie Meld**: Add the cabbage, sweet potato, and rice cakes. Stir continuously, allowing the vegetables to wilt and the rice cakes to become soft and chewy, absorbing the spicy chicken juices.
4. **Communal Finish**: Top with torn perilla leaves and toasted sesame seeds. Serve directly from the skillet, encouraging everyone to dig in together.
imagePrompt: Overhead food photography of Dakgalbi in a massive black cast-iron skillet. Vibrant red gochujang sauce coating chicken and rice cakes. Wisps of steam, scattered green perilla leaves, professional lighting.

title: Muhammara (Roasted Red Pepper & Walnut Dip)
description: A silken, high-vibe dip from the Levant, Muhammara is a masterclass in balance. The smoky sweetness of charred red peppers meets the earthy crunch of walnuts, all tied together with the sharp, acidic tang of pomegranate molasses.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Red bell peppers","qty":"3","unit":"large","notes":"roasted and peeled"},{"name":"Walnuts","qty":"1","unit":"cup","notes":"lightly toasted"},{"name":"Pomegranate molasses","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for that essential zing"},{"name":"Aleppo pepper","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"mild, fruity heat"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Char the Peppers**: Roast red bell peppers over an open flame or under a broiler until the skins are blackened and blistered. Peel away the skin and remove the seeds to reveal the tender, smoky flesh.
2. **Pulse to Perfection**: In a food processor, combine the peppers, toasted walnuts, pomegranate molasses, and a pinch of cumin. Pulse until the mixture is thick and slightly textured, not a perfectly smooth puree.
3. **Emulsify**: With the motor running, slowly drizzle in extra virgin olive oil until the dip becomes glossy and unctuous.
4. **Garnish**: Spread on a shallow plate, create a swirl with a spoon, and fill the well with more pomegranate molasses and a few crushed walnuts. Serve with warm, charred pita.
imagePrompt: Close-up shot of Muhammara dip in a hand-crafted stone bowl. Deep rustic red color, topped with a swirl of dark pomegranate molasses and whole walnuts. Warm afternoon sunlight, high contrast.

title: Lomo Saltado (Peruvian Beef Stir-fry)
description: A dazzling collision of Cantonese technique and Andean ingredients, Lomo Saltado is the jewel of Peru's Chifa tradition. High-heat-seared beef strips dance with crisp red onions and tomatoes, served with both fluffy rice and golden french fries.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Beef tenderloin","qty":"400","unit":"g","notes":"sliced into thin strips"},{"name":"Aji Amarillo paste","qty":"1","unit":"tbsp","notes":"Peruvian yellow chili paste"},{"name":"Red onion","qty":"1","unit":"large","notes":"thickly sliced"},{"name":"French fries","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"crispy and hot"},{"name":"Soy sauce","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for the Chifa touch"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Flash Sear**: In a screaming-hot wok or heavy skillet, sear the beef strips in small batches. You want a dark, smoky crust while keeping the centers tender and pink.
2. **The Aromatic Lift**: Toss in the red onions and Aji Amarillo paste. The onions should soften slightly but retain their vibrant crunch. Add the tomatoes at the very last second so they warm through without collapsing.
3. **The Deglaze**: Pour in the soy sauce and a splash of vinegar. The liquid will flash-vaporize, creating a rich 'wok hei' aroma and a glossy sauce that coats every element.
4. **The Texture Play**: Fold in the crispy french fries just before serving. This ensures they absorb some of the savory juices while remaining pleasantly crisp. Serve alongside a dome of white rice.
imagePrompt: Action shot of Lomo Saltado being tossed in a wok. Flaming beef strips, purple onions, and golden fries. High-speed culinary photography, steam, sparks, vibrant colors.

title: Lamb Rogan Josh (Kashmiri Slow-Cooked Lamb)
description: A regal masterpiece from the mountains of Kashmir, Rogan Josh is a slow-cooked embrace of aromatic spices. Tender lamb chunks are braised in a velvety sauce colored deep red by dried Kashmiri chilies and fragranced with cardamom and cloves.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Lamb shoulder","qty":"800","unit":"g","notes":"cut into large cubes"},{"name":"Kashmiri chili powder","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for deep red color without extreme heat"},{"name":"Yogurt (Full fat)","qty":"150","unit":"g","notes":"whisked smooth"},{"name":"Whole spices","qty":"1","unit":"set","notes":"Cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Searing and Infusing**: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed Dutch oven. Sear the lamb cubes until brown and crusty. Remove and fry the whole spices until they release their floral, woody oils.
2. **The Color Ritual**: Whisk the Kashmiri chili powder and ginger powder into the yogurt. This creates the 'Rogan'—the vibrant red oil that defines the dish.
3. **The Slow Braise**: Return the lamb to the pot and stir it into the yogurt base. Cover and cook on a very low simmer for 75 minutes. The lamb should become exceptionally tender, surrendering to the fork.
4. **Final Reduction**: Uncover and increase the heat slightly to thicken the sauce until it's rich and clings to the meat. Finish with a handful of fresh cilantro and serve with buttery naan.
imagePrompt: Overhead food photography of Lamb Rogan Josh in a copper karahi. Deep crimson gravy, tender meat, garnish of cilantro. Warm low lighting, rustic Indian setting, 8k resolution.

title: Spicy Tuna Crispy Rice
description: A modern Izakaya favorite that masterfully plays with temperature and texture. Golden-fried cubes of sushi rice serve as a crunchy pedestal for chilled, spicy-creamy tuna, finished with a slice of jalapeño for a sharp, clean heat.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Sushi Grade Tuna","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"finely minced"},{"name":"Spicy Mayo","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"Kewpie mayo + Sriracha"},{"name":"Sushi Rice","qty":"2","unit":"cups","notes":"cooked and seasoned"},{"name":"Jalapeño","qty":"1","unit":"pc","notes":"thinly sliced"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Rice Foundations**: Press the seasoned sushi rice into a rectangular mold and chill until firm. Once cold, cut into uniform bite-sized cubes.
2. **The Golden Fry**: Shallow fry the rice cubes in high-heat oil until they develop a thick, golden, 'glassy' crunch on all sides. Drain on paper towels to maintain the crispness.
3. **Tuna Preparation**: Finely mince the tuna and fold it gently into the spicy mayo and a touch of sesame oil. Keep this mixture chilled until the very last second.
4. **Assembly**: Peak a generous spoonful of spicy tuna onto each crispy rice cube. Top with a single jalapeño slice and a dot of sriracha. Serve immediately while the rice is still warm and the tuna is ice-cold.
imagePrompt: Close-up macro shot of three Spicy Tuna Crispy Rice pieces on a slate platter. Visible golden rice texture, vibrant pink tuna, glistening jalapeño. High-end sushi restaurant aesthetic.

title: Ceviche Mixto (Peruvian Mixed Seafood Ceviche)
description: The ultimate expression of coastal freshness, Ceviche Mixto is a vibrant medley of sea bass, shrimp, and squid cured in sharp 'Leche de Tigre'. It's a bracingly cold, citrusy explosion balanced by the creamy sweetness of camote (sweet potato).
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Sea Bass fillet","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"firm, white fish, cubed"},{"name":"Shrimp","qty":"150","unit":"g","notes":"peeled and blanched"},{"name":"Squid rings","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"tenderized"},{"name":"Lime juice","qty":"200","unit":"ml","notes":"freshly squeezed"},{"name":"Cilantro","qty":"1","unit":"bunch","notes":"finely chopped"},{"name":"Sweet potato (Camote)","qty":"1","unit":"large","notes":"boiled and sliced"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Leche de Tigre**: Whisk fresh lime juice with a touch of fish stock, minced garlic, ginger, and a piece of celery. Blend and strain to create a perfectly clear, bracingly sharp curing liquid.
2. **The Cure**: Toss the fish, shrimp, and squid in the Leche de Tigre with a handful of sliced red onions and chilies. Let it sit for exactly 3-5 minutes—you want the fish to turn opaque on the outside while remaining silky and translucent in the center.
3. **Ice-Cold Balance**: Gently fold in fresh cilantro and a touch of ice to keep the ceviche at a freezing temperature throughout the serving process.
4. **Plating**: Serve in a chilled glass bowl with disks of boiled sweet potato and crunchy 'cancha' (corn nuts) on the side to provide a sweet and textural counterpoint to the sharp acidity.
imagePrompt: Top-down view of Ceviche Mixto in a crystal bowl. Translucent fish, bright green cilantro, slivers of red onion. Glistening leche de tigre sauce. High-end beach resort style.

title: Bún Bò Huế (Spicy Vietnamese Beef Noodle Soup)
description: The bold, spicy cousin of Pho, Bún Bò Huế is the pride of Central Vietnam. A complex broth infused with lemongrass and shrimp paste, served with thick vermicelli noodles and tender slices of beef shank and ham.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Beef shank","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"slow-cooked until tender"},{"name":"Pork bones","qty":"1","unit":"kg","notes":"for the rich broth base"},{"name":"Lemongrass","qty":"6","unit":"stalks","notes":"bruised for maximum aroma"},{"name":"Bun vermicelli","qty":"400","unit":"g","notes":"thick, round noodles"},{"name":"Shrimp paste (Mam Nem)","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"the soul of the dish"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Royal Broth**: Simmer pork bones and beef shank with bruised lemongrass stalks for at least 3 hours. Skim consistently to maintain a deep, clear, but intensely flavored foundation.
2. **The Spicy Bloom**: In a small pan, fry annatto seeds and chili flakes in oil until the fat turns a brilliant, fiery orange. Whisk in the fermented shrimp paste until the aroma becomes savory and deep.
3. **Broth Infusion**: Strain the spicy oil into the main broth. The liquid should transform into a beautiful, crimson-flecked soup with a balanced lemongrass-umami scent.
4. **Assembly**: Place thick vermicelli noodles in a large pre-warmed bowl. Top with slices of beef shank, sprigs of mint, and paper-thin red cabbage. Pour the boiling spicy broth over the top and serve with a squeeze of lime.
imagePrompt: Steam rising from a bowl of Bún Bò Huế. Fiery orange-red broth, thick white noodles, slices of beef, fresh mint. Moody street photography style, 8k resolution.

title: Spanakopita (Greek Spinach & Feta Pie)
description: A golden, shattering-crisp masterpiece of Greek home cooking. Layers of paper-thin phyllo pastry are brushed with olive oil and filled with a vibrantly green, savory mix of spinach, feta cheese, and fragrant dill.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Phyllo pastry","qty":"1","unit":"pack","notes":"thawed to room temperature"},{"name":"Spinach (Fresh)","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"washed and squeezed dry"},{"name":"Feta cheese","qty":"250","unit":"g","notes":"crumbled into large chunks"},{"name":"Fresh Dill","qty":"1","unit":"bunch","notes":"finely chopped"},{"name":"Olive oil","qty":"100","unit":"ml","notes":"for brushing between layers"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Green Preparation**: Sauté the fresh spinach until wilted. This is the crucial step: you MUST squeeze the spinach in a kitchen towel until it's nearly dry. Any moisture will result in a soggy crust.
2. **The Filling**: In a large bowl, combine the dry spinach with crumbled feta, fresh dill, and a pinch of nutmeg. The feta should be in varied chunks to provide surprises of salty creaminess.
3. **Phyllo Engineering**: Lay a sheet of phyllo in a baking dish and brush generously with olive oil. Repeat for 6-8 layers. Spread the spinach mixture evenly.
4. **The Top Crust**: Layer another 6 sheets of phyllo on top, oiling each one carefully. Score the top layer into squares with a sharp knife to allow steam to escape. Bake at 180°C until the pastry is a deep, shattery, golden brown.
imagePrompt: Close up of a slice of Spanakopita. Golden flaky phyllo pastry layers, visible green spinach and white feta filling. Dusting of crumbs on a wooden table. Warm kitchen lighting.

title: Merguez avec Couscous (Spicy Moroccan Sausage & Grains)
description: A smoky, aromatic North African classic. Fiery Merguez lamb sausages are grilled to a snap and served over a mountain of fluffy, butter-flecked couscous with a side of harissa-spiced vegetable tagine.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Merguez sausages","qty":"6","unit":"pcs","notes":"spicy lamb and beef"},{"name":"Semolina couscous","qty":"250","unit":"g","notes":"steamed to puffiness"},{"name":"Harissa paste","qty":"1","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for the tagine base"},{"name":"Chickpeas","qty":"1","unit":"can","notes":"rinsed and drained"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Grains**: Place couscous in a heatproof bowl with a pinch of salt and a knob of butter. Cover with boiling water and seal tightly for 10 minutes. Fluff with a fork—every grain should be separate and light.
2. **The Vegetable Bed**: Sauté peppers, carrots, and chickpeas with harissa and a touch of cumin. Add a splash of water to create a light, spicy 'jus' that will soak into the couscous.
3. **The Grill**: Prick the sausages and grill over high heat until they are slightly charred and the spicy red fat begins to render. The skin should be tense and snap when bitten.
4. **Plating**: Mound the couscous on a large platter, create a well for the vegetables, and crown with the hot, smoky Merguez sausages. Garnish with fresh mint and a squeeze of lemon.
imagePrompt: Moroccan table setting with a platter of Merguez and couscous. Vibrant colors, steam, fresh mint, silver tea set in the background. Professional culinary photography.

title: Pollo a la Brasa (Peruvian Roasted Chicken)
description: The soul of Peruvian rotisserie, Pollo a la Brasa is defined by its deep, dark, flavorful crust. Marinated in a complex blend of soy sauce, cumin, and dark beer, the chicken is roasted until the skin is perfectly crisp and the meat is fall-apart tender.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Whole chicken","qty":"1.5","unit":"kg","notes":"patted dry for crispy skin"},{"name":"Dark beer (Porter or Stout)","qty":"100","unit":"ml","notes":"the secret tenderizer"},{"name":"Soy sauce","qty":"50","unit":"ml","notes":"for salt and color"},{"name":"Aji Panca paste","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"Peruvian smoky chili paste"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Deep Marinade**: Whisk together the dark beer, soy sauce, cumin, and aji panca. Rub the chicken thoroughly, even under the skin. Let this marinate for at least 6 hours—overnight is divine.
2. **The Prep**: Remove the chicken from the fridge 30 minutes before roasting to ensure it cooks evenly. Truss the legs to keep the breast meat moist.
3. **The Roast**: Place in a 200°C oven. The sugar in the beer and soy will create an incredibly dark, lacquered brown skin. Baste every 20 minutes with the rendered juices.
4. **Rest and Serve**: Let the chicken rest for 15 minutes before carving—this is non-negotiable for juicy results. Serve with the iconic 'Aji Verde' (green chili sauce) and thick-cut fries.
imagePrompt: Whole roasted Pollo a la Brasa being carved on a wooden board. Dark mahogany crispy skin, glistening meat. Garnish of lime and fresh herbs. Moody rustic lighting.

title: Katsu Curry (Japanese Panko Pork with Curry)
description: A comforting Japanese comfort-food staple that balances the ultimate crunch with a silky, aromatic sauce. A thick, panko-crusted pork cutlet is deep-fried to golden perfection and served over a bed of short-grain rice with a mild, vegetable-rich curry gravy.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Pork loin cutlet","qty":"2","unit":"pcs","notes":"pounded flat for even frying"},{"name":"Panko breadcrumbs","qty":"1","unit":"cup","notes":"use high quality Japanese panko"},{"name":"Japanese Curry Roux","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"e.g. S&B or Vermont Curry"},{"name":"Short-grain rice","qty":"2","unit":"cups","notes":"steamed until fluffy"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Golden Crust**: Pound the pork cutlets until they are about 1cm thick. Coat in flour, then whisked egg, and finally firmly press into the flaky panko. This 3-step process ensures a crust that never falls off.
2. **Curry Foundation**: Sauté onions until translucent, then add carrots and potatoes. Simmer in water until tender. Whisk in the curry roux blocks until the sauce is thick, glossy, and smells of mild, sweet spices.
3. **The Deep Fry**: Fry the cutlets in 175°C oil for 3-4 minutes per side. The panko should become a vibrant, shattered golden-brown. Let it rest on a rack to stay crisp.
4. **Plating**: Slice the tonkatsu into thick strips. Pour a generous amount of curry sauce on one side of a deep bowl, place the rice on the other, and lay the crispy pork directly on the dividing line. Serve with pickled fukujinzuke.
imagePrompt: High-angle shot of Katsu Curry in a deep white bowl. Golden panko cutlet slices, dark brown curry with visible carrot cubes, white fluffy rice. Studio lighting, professional culinary presentation.

title: Tabbouleh (Levantine Herb & Bulgur Salad)
description: The green heart of the Levant, Tabbouleh is less a grain salad and more an herb celebration. Finely minced flat-leaf parsley and mint form the base, accented by a touch of nutty bulgur and a sharp, lemony dressing.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Flat leaf parsley","qty":"3","unit":"bunches","notes":"finely minced (NOT processed)"},{"name":"Fine Bulgur","qty":"1/4","unit":"cup","notes":"soaked in lemon juice"},{"name":"Fresh Mint","qty":"1","unit":"bunch","notes":"finely chopped"},{"name":"Extra virgin olive oil","qty":"3","unit":"tbsp","notes":"highest quality"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Mincing Magic**: This is the chef's secret: use a very sharp knife to mince the parsley by hand. Never use a food processor, as it bruises and wets the herbs. The parsley should be like green snow.
2. **The Bulgur Soak**: Rinse the fine bulgur and let it soak directly in fresh lemon juice and a touch of salt. This allows the grain to absorb the bright acidity from the inside out.
3. **Tomato Prep**: Deseed the tomatoes and dice them into tiny, uniform cubes. Let them drain for a few minutes so their juice doesn't drown the herbs.
4. **The Big Toss**: Mix the parsley, mint, tomatoes, and soaked bulgur. Drizzle with a generous amount of olive oil. The result should be a mountain of vibrant green that tastes like a cool Mediterranean garden.
imagePrompt: Macro shot of a bowl of Tabbouleh. Vibrant green minced parsley, flecks of red tomato, hydrated bulgur grains. Glistening olive oil, rustic ceramic bowl, daylight.

title: Pastel de Choclo (Chilean Corn Pie)
description: A soulful, sweet-and-savory comfort food from the heart of Chile. A rich ground beef and onion base (pino) is topped with a layer of creamy, basil-scented corn mash, baked until it develops a caramelized, golden crust.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Sweet corn","qty":"6","unit":"ears","notes":"grated to a creamy pulp"},{"name":"Ground beef","qty":"400","unit":"g","notes":"sautéed with onions"},{"name":"Black olives","qty":"50","unit":"g","notes":"whole, pitted"},{"name":"Fresh basil","qty":"1","unit":"handful","notes":"blended into the corn"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Pino base**: Brown ground beef with a generous amount of onions, cumin, and paprika. Traditionally, a few raisins and a whole hard-boiled egg are tucked into the corner for 'luck'.
2. **The Corn Crust**: Grate fresh corn cob by cob to get the milky starch. Cook this pulp in a pot with butter and a blender-full of fresh basil until it thickens into a heavy, sweet-savory custard.
3. **Assembly**: Layer the beef mixture in a clay baking dish. Top it with the basil-corn mash. Sprinkle the top with a tiny bit of sugar to help it caramelize in the oven.
4. **Baking**: Bake at 200°C until the edges are bubbling and the top is a beautiful, mottled golden-brown. Rest for 10 minutes before serving—it's magma-hot otherwise!
imagePrompt: Pastel de Choclo in a traditional brown clay dish. Caramelized golden-brown top, steam rising. Garnish of fresh basil leaf. Warm rustic lighting.

title: Banh Mi (Vietnamese Baguette Sandwich)
description: The world's most sophisticated sandwich. A light, airy Vietnamese baguette filled with savory roasted pork, creamy pâté, and a crisp, chilled forest of pickled vegetables, cilantro, and fiery chilies.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Pork shoulder","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"thinly sliced and grilled"},{"name":"Vietnamese baguette","qty":"2","unit":"pcs","notes":"extra light and crispy"},{"name":"Pickled Daikon & Carrot","qty":"1","unit":"cup","notes":"vinegary and sweet"},{"name":"Liver pâté","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for depth and richness"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Bread Revive**: Briefly toast the baguettes in a hot oven for 2 minutes. The goal is a crust that 'shatters' while the crumb remains like a cloud.
2. **The Spread**: Spread a generous layer of creamy pâté on the bottom and a swipe of mayonnaise on the top. This fat layer protects the bread from the juicy pork.
3. **The Filling Order**: Layer the savory grilled pork first, then add the pickled daikon and carrots for acidic contrast. Finish with long slivers of cucumber and whole cilantro sprigs.
4. **The Final Kick**: Add a few rounds of fresh bird's eye chili and a dash of Maggi seasoning. Close and compress slightly so the flavors begin to meld. Wrap in butcher paper for the authentic street experience.
imagePrompt: Side view of a loaded Banh Mi sandwich on brown paper. Visible layers of pork, cilantro sprigs, and bright red chilies. Vibrant street-food vibe, high resolution.

title: Tajine de Daurade (Moroccan Fish Tagine)
description: A coastal Moroccan treasure. Whole sea bream is slow-cooked in a conical clay tagine with 'Chermoula'—a bold, herbaceous paste of cilantro, garlic, lemon, and paprika, layered over a foundation of tender potatoes and tomatoes.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Sea bream (Whole)","qty":"1","unit":"kg","notes":"scored for marinating"},{"name":"Chermoula paste","qty":"3","unit":"tbsp","notes":"Cilantro, garlic, lemon, cumin"},{"name":"Preserved lemon","qty":"1","unit":"half","notes":"finely diced"},{"name":"Potatoes & Carrots","qty":"2","unit":"each","notes":"sliced into rounds"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Chermoula Lab**: Pound cilantro, garlic, cumin, and paprika into a thick paste. Whisk in a massive amount of fresh lemon juice and olive oil. Rub this into the scored flesh of the fish and let it rest.
2. **Base Layering**: In the bottom of the tagine, layer disks of carrots and potatoes. These act as a 'trivet' for the fish, preventing it from sticking while absorbing all the aromatic juices.
3. **The Construct**: Place the marinated sea bream on top of the vegetables. Top with slices of tomato and diced preserved lemon. Pour any remaining chermoula over the top.
4. **Conical Magic**: Cover with the tagine lid and cook over a low flame. The conical shape circulates the steam, keeping the fish incredibly moist and tender. Serve directly from the tagine with crusty bread to sop up the herbal sauce.
imagePrompt: Moroccan fish tagine being opened, revealing a whole sea bream inside. Steam, vibrant red and green colors from tomatoes and cilantro. Traditional clay tagine, intricate patterns.
